title: Multi-Module Java Coursework (Data Structures & Algorithms)
summary: Repository containing homework, labs, and projects. Each subdirectory is an independent Java module with its own build and test infrastructure. Stubs marked with TODO must be implemented across 22 logical units spanning data structures, algorithms, tile rendering, and game logic.
instructionPreview: This task is from an undergraduate data-structures programming sequence. You must implement stub functions across multiple modules in this multi-project Java codebase. Every function marked with `throw new UnsupportedOperationException("TODO: implement")` or `// TODO: Implement` needs a correct implementation.
instruction:
This task is from an undergraduate data-structures programming sequence. You must implement
stub functions across multiple modules in this multi-project Java codebase. Every function
marked with `throw new UnsupportedOperationException("TODO: implement")` or
`// TODO: Implement` needs a correct implementation.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present.

## Modules to implement

### proj0 — 2048 Game Logic (`proj0/src/game2048logic/`)
- **GameLogic.java**: Core tile-movement mechanics for a 2048 sliding-puzzle game.
  The board is a 2D int array (0 = empty). Implement single-tile sliding with merge,
  per-column tilt, full-board tilt, and directional tilt using board rotations.
- **MatrixUtils.java**: In-place 90-degree rotation of square integer matrices
  (both left/counter-clockwise and right/clockwise).

### proj0 — 2048 Rendering Engine (`proj0/src/game2048rendering/`)
- **Model.java**: Game state wrapper that checks for empty spaces, max-tile win
  condition, valid-move existence, and interpolates board moves for animation.
  Includes a NumberTracker helper class for tracking tile weights during move
  interpolation, plus board copying and perspective adjustment.

### proj0_hardmode — Hard Mode 2048 (`proj0_hardmode/src/`)
- Same structure as proj0. Implement MatrixUtils (rotations) and Model (game state,
  move interpolation, tile queue building, NumberTracker, perspective adjustment).

### lab03 — Adventure Game (`lab03/src/adventure/`)
- **AdventureGame.java**: Text adventure game engine — stage handling, response
  parsing, game loop, and string representation.
- **BeeCountingStage.java**: Stage where the player counts bees in a string and
  sums digit inputs.
- **PalindromeStage.java**: Stage for palindrome detection using digit-to-list
  conversion and list reversal.
- **SpeciesListStage.java**: Stage that handles species list responses and computes
  array similarity scores.
- **MachineStage.java**: Stage implementing element-wise max, array sum, array max,
  and sum-of-elementwise-max operations.
- **IntList.java** (`lab03/src/common/`): Singly-linked integer list with get-by-index,
  factory construction from varargs, equality check, and print.

### lab06 — Unordered Linked List Map (`lab06/src/`)
- **ULLMap.java**: Map backed by an unordered linked list of key-value entries.
  Implement get, put, containsKey, and an iterator (hasNext/next).

### lab09/lab10/proj3 — Tile Engine (`src/tileengine/`)
- **TETile.java**: Immutable tile class for a 2D tile-based world. Implement
  drawing (with image fallback), color variation, 2D-array-to-string conversion,
  deep copy of tile arrays, and equality by ID.
- **TERenderer.java**: Tile rendering engine — initialize StdDraw canvas with
  offsets, render a 2D tile array frame, and draw tiles to canvas.

### lab09/lab10/proj3 — Random Utilities (`src/utils/`)
- **RandomUtils.java**: Pseudo-random number generation library. Implement uniform
  long sampling, Gaussian (Box-Muller), Poisson (Knuth), discrete sampling from
  probability/frequency arrays, Fisher-Yates shuffle (4 type variants + 3 subarray
  variants), and random permutation generation.

### lab10 — Tetris Movement (`lab10/src/tetris/`)
- **Movement.java**: Tetris piece movement — try-move with boundary/collision
  detection, drop-down, rotation feasibility check, and piece rotation.

### lab11 — Sorting Algorithms (`lab11/src/`)
- **SelectionSort.java**: In-place selection sort with a min-finding helper.
- **InsertionSort.java**: In-place insertion sort.

### hw2 — Percolation Statistics (`hw2/src/`)
- **PercolationStats.java**: Monte Carlo simulation for estimating the percolation
  threshold. Implement the constructor (run T trials on N×N grids), plus 95%
  confidence interval endpoints.

moduleDag.description: Repository containing homework, labs, and projects. Each subdirectory is an independent Java module with its own build and test infrastructure. Stubs marked with TODO must be implemented across 22 logical units spanning data structures, algorithms, tile rendering, and game logic.

nodes.label: HW2 — Percolation Statistics
nodes.description: Monte Carlo simulation estimating the percolation threshold on an N×N grid. Runs T independent trials using the pre-existing Percolation class and computes sample mean, stddev, and 95% confidence interval endpoints.

nodes.label: Lab03 — IntList (common data structure)
nodes.description: Singly-linked integer list used by PalindromeStage. Provides index-based get, varargs factory construction (IntList.of), structural equality check, and a recursive string printer.

nodes.label: Lab03 — Independent Adventure Stages (Bee, Machine, Species)
nodes.description: Three self-contained text-adventure puzzle stages with no dependency on IntList. BeeCountingStage counts bee glyphs and sums digit inputs; MachineStage implements bitwise max/add helpers and sum-of-elementwise-max; SpeciesListStage computes array similarity scores over animal name lists.

nodes.label: Lab03 — PalindromeStage
nodes.description: Adventure stage that prompts for a palindromic room number, converts the input string to an IntList via digitsToIntList, reverses it, and uses IntList.equals to check palindrome property.

nodes.label: Lab03 — AdventureGame engine
nodes.description: Top-level text-adventure game loop. Dispatches to stage playStage(), parses player responses (exact then substring match) against the stage's response map, advances the current stage, and terminates when no responses remain.

nodes.label: Lab06 — ULLMap (Unordered Linked List Map)
nodes.description: Generic map backed by an unordered singly-linked list of key-value Entry nodes. Implements get, put (with in-place update), containsKey, and an Iterator (hasNext/next) by traversing the Entry chain.

nodes.label: Lab09 — RandomUtils
nodes.description: Pseudo-random number utility library. Implements uniform long sampling, Gaussian (Box-Muller), Poisson (Knuth), discrete sampling from probability/frequency arrays, Fisher-Yates shuffle in four type variants plus three subarray variants, and random permutation generation.

nodes.label: Lab09 — TETile
nodes.description: Immutable tile for a 2D tile-based world. Implements draw (image filepath with character-color fallback), colorVariant (uses RandomUtils.uniform for per-channel jitter), 2D-array-to-string, deep copy of tile arrays, and equality by integer id.

nodes.label: Lab09 — TERenderer
nodes.description: StdDraw-based tile rendering engine. Initialises the canvas with width, height, and x/y offsets; renders a full frame by clearing the canvas and calling TETile.draw for every cell in the 2D world array.

nodes.label: Lab10 — RandomUtils
nodes.description: Identical pseudo-random library to lab09's RandomUtils, copied into the lab10 module. Must be implemented before TETile.colorVariant is usable within lab10's build.

nodes.label: Lab10 — TETile
nodes.description: Identical TETile implementation to lab09, copied into lab10's build. draw, colorVariant (calls lab10's RandomUtils.uniform), toString, copyOf, and equals by id must all be filled in before Movement can check board tiles.

nodes.label: Lab10 — TERenderer
nodes.description: Identical TERenderer to lab09, copied into lab10. Initialises the StdDraw canvas and renders frames by delegating to TETile.draw for every cell.

nodes.label: Lab10 — Tetris Movement
nodes.description: Tetris piece movement controller. tryMove applies delta with boundary and collision detection (comparing board cells against TETile Tileset.NOTHING via TETile.equals); dropDown places a piece; canRotate checks the rotated shape fits; rotate applies left/right rotation matrices.

nodes.label: Lab11 — Sorting Algorithms (Selection + Insertion)
nodes.description: In-place comparison sorts over generic Comparable arrays. SelectionSort repeatedly finds the minimum of the unsorted suffix and swaps it into place; InsertionSort inserts each element into its correct position in the sorted prefix.

nodes.label: proj0 — MatrixUtils (2D matrix rotations)
nodes.description: In-place 90-degree rotation of a square int[][] matrix. rotateLeft (counter-clockwise): transpose then reverse each column. rotateRight (clockwise): transpose then reverse each row. Used by GameLogic.tilt to reframe directional tilts as upward tilts.

nodes.label: proj0 — GameLogic (tile sliding and tilt)
nodes.description: Core 2048 mechanics: moveTileUpAsFarAsPossible slides and optionally merges a single tile; tiltColumn processes one column upward; tiltUp applies tiltColumn across all columns; tilt rotates the board via MatrixUtils so that any of the four directional tilts becomes an upward tilt.

nodes.label: proj0 — Model (game state and rendering bridge)
nodes.description: Game state wrapper for the rendering layer. Implements emptySpaceExists, maxTileExists, atLeastOneMoveExists, move (delegates to GameLogic.tilt), board copy/perspective adjustment, and move-interpolation helpers including a NumberTracker inner class for tracking tile weights during animation.

nodes.label: proj0_hardmode — MatrixUtils (2D matrix rotations)
nodes.description: Same rotateLeft/rotateRight implementation as proj0, copied into the hardmode module. Must be present before Model can compile its direct static imports of rotateLeft and rotateRight.

nodes.label: proj0_hardmode — Model (hard-mode game state)
nodes.description: Extended Model for hard-mode 2048. Directly imports MatrixUtils.rotateLeft/ rotateRight and calls the pre-existing GameLogic.tilt. Adds tile queue building, extended move interpolation, NumberTracker inner class, and perspective adjustment on top of the base proj0 Model logic.

nodes.label: proj3 — RandomUtils
nodes.description: Same pseudo-random library as lab09/lab10, copied into proj3's build. Must be in place before TETile.colorVariant can use RandomUtils.uniform within proj3's compilation unit.

nodes.label: proj3 — TETile
nodes.description: Identical TETile to lab09 and lab10, resident in proj3's source tree. draw, colorVariant (uses proj3's RandomUtils.uniform), toString, copyOf, and equals by id must be implemented before proj3's TERenderer can call TETile.draw.

nodes.label: proj3 — TERenderer
nodes.description: Identical TERenderer to lab09 and lab10, resident in proj3's source tree. Initialises the StdDraw canvas and renders tile frames by iterating over the world array and calling TETile.draw on each cell.

edges.label: PalindromeStage calls IntList.of() to build digit lists and IntList.equals() to check palindrome property
AdventureGame.handleStage() calls playStage() on BeeCountingStage, MachineStage, and SpeciesListStage instances
AdventureGame.handleStage() calls playStage() on PalindromeStage; the stage must be functional first
TETile.colorVariant calls RandomUtils.uniform(r, -dv, dv+1) for per-channel colour jitter
TERenderer.drawTiles iterates the world array and calls world[x][y].draw() on each TETile
TETile.colorVariant calls RandomUtils.uniform(r, -dv, dv+1) within lab10's build
TERenderer.drawTiles calls world[x][y].draw() which requires TETile.draw to be implemented
Movement.canMove compares board cells to Tileset.NOTHING using TETile.equals; requires TETile.equals implemented
GameLogic.tilt calls MatrixUtils.rotateLeft/rotateRight to reframe non-north tilts as upward tilts
Model.move calls GameLogic.tilt(after, side) to apply the tile-sliding logic to the board
Model directly imports and calls MatrixUtils.rotateLeft/rotateRight for board perspective adjustment
TETile.colorVariant calls RandomUtils.uniform(r, -dv, dv+1) within proj3's build
TERenderer.drawTiles calls world[x][y].draw() which requires TETile.draw to be implemented
